texto: Tied to the physical, deaf to the eternal, riveted by my own shortcomings, I was thinking only of what a bad choice I'd made when choosing a partner for a chat. This guy was faking timidity to lure someone over. If I said victim, he was likely to start gnawing my neck. If I said vampire, he would demand proof. I hadn't the fangs enough to back that pretension.
explicacao: Citação de Christine Wicker sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Tied to the physical, deaf to the eternal, riveted by my own shortcomings, I was thinking only of…” articula reflexao, vampires, physical em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: Tied to the physical, deaf to the eternal, riveted by my own shortcomings, I was thinking only of what a bad choice I'd made when choosing a partner for a chat. This guy was faking timidity to lure someone over. If I said victim, he was likely to start gnawing my neck. If I said vampire, he would demand proof. I hadn't the fangs enough to back that pretension.

texto: They're power-hungry, the mundane said of the magical people. They're immoral, people said, and they're scary. Playing with the dark arts could plunge me into evil. I'd be pulled toward depravity. Blasphemy would begin to seem like truth, bad like good, God like Satan. It had happened to people through the centuries, they said. And they were right. All that did happen.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Christine Wicker: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “They're power-hungry, the mundane said of the magical people. They're immoral, people said, and t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, dangerous-people, evil pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e evil.
